action.reasoning: Observation: Current best is 0.6537 bal_acc (iter24, RN50 cRT: Focal(gamma=2)+sqrt-sampler+mix/cut-rand-p0.5+RandAug+AdamW5e-4-cos s1=60ep val~0.56, CE+count-sampler+SGD0.05-nest+reinit-head+freeze s2=40ep lift to ~0.583, post-tau=1.7 grid val=0.644). Last run (27) only reached val=0.55/tau-val=0.608 and test bal_acc=0.6153 due to incomplete/buggy truncated train.py (stage2 no lift, likely missing head reinit/balanced sampler/proper freeze/SGD). Model/predict stable (CIFAR stem RN50, weights_only=False, batched norm+logit-adj, metrics.jsonl). Analysis: Exact replication of iter24 config + explicit fc reinit/named_param freeze/offset steps for logging/per-epoch val after every stage + label_smoothing=0.1 in s2 CE will recover >0.65 (curves showed stable convergence). ConvNeXt switch risky (stem/featmap mismatch on 32x32, prior low score). With 3 iters/1.17h left (~6min/iter), one strong run+eval suffices. Decision: Overwrite train.py with complete clean code matching best config (seeds, full loops, mix/cut rand 50% in s1, proper s2 reinit/freeze/opt/scheduler/best-save across stages+tau grid 0-3 step 0.05, per-20step loss + per-epoch val logging with stage, no AutoAug), keep model/predict; run train then submit_eval on best.pt.
